name: torrent_scanner
short_description: Fast utility to index .torrent files and match them against on-disk folders/files with SQLite storage.
long_description: torrent_scanner is a local utility that indexes BitTorrent .torrent files and intelligently matches them against data on disk. It provides both a high-level Python API for library integration and a comprehensive command-line interface. The tool uses bencode parsing to extract torrent metadata, stores data in SQLite with Peewee ORM, and leverages embedded Redis (redislite) for fast lookups. It supports matching both single-file and multi-file torrents through name-based and structure-based hashing, handles permission errors gracefully, and skips symlinks during scanning. Multiple export formats (JSON, JSONL, CSV) are available for integration with other tools.

modules.description: High-level public API providing the TorrentScanner class and convenience functions (quick_check, quick_locate, quick_identify) for library use.
Command-line interface with organized commands for indexing, matching, querying, and managing torrent metadata with multiple output formats.
Core scanning orchestration logic that uses Redis for fast lookups and supports both file-based and structure-based torrent matching.
Torrent file parsing and metadata extraction implementing BEP-3 torrent format with SHA-1 info hash computation.
Minimal bencode decoder with offset tracking for parsing BEP-3 torrent files and extracting the info dictionary span.
Database models using Peewee ORM defining Torrent, TorrentFile, and Match tables with indexes for efficient querying.
Filesystem utilities for discovering .torrent files, computing structure hashes for directories, and computing file hashes.
Package initialization exporting the public API including TorrentScanner class, models, parsing functions, and CLI entry point.
